import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
      i = i + 1
    else
      mut sign = 1
      if b == 45
        sign = -1
        i = i + 1
      else if b == 43
        i = i + 1
      end
      mut val = 0
      mut parsing = true
      while parsing
        if i >= n
          parsing = false
        else
          let c = match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(x)
            x
          when None
            0
          end
          if c >= 48 and c <= 57
            val = val * 10 + (c - 48)
            i = i + 1
          else
            parsing = false
          end
        end
      end
      result = list.push(result, val * sign)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn binary_search(_ xs: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len()
  mut found: Option<Int> = None
  mut done = false
  while not done
    if lo >= hi
      done = true
    else
      let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
      match xs.get(mid)
      when Some(v)
        if v == target
          found = Some(mid)
          done = true
        else if v < target
          lo = mid + 1
        else
          hi = mid
        end
      when None
        done = true
      end
    end
  end
  found
end

fn run() -> Unit
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line1)
    match io.read_line()
    when Some(line2)
      let xs = parse_line(line1)
      match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
      when Some(t)
        match binary_search(xs, t)
        when Some(idx)
          println("index=#{idx}")
        when None
          println("not_found")
        end
      when None
        println("not_found")
      end
    when None
      println("not_found")
    end
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
end

run()
